one_liner: CELH flat at $29.79 — Wyckoff flags Accumulation Phase A-B with DCF showing 34.9% upside; consolidating tightly near the midpoint of the BB with volume drying up.

price_action.intraday_story: Tight range session on below-average volume — exactly the volume dry-up pattern that precedes a VCP breakout in Wyckoff accumulation setups.

news_catalyst.headline: No Significant News — Energy Drink Sector Stable
news_catalyst.summary: No material CELH news. Stock is in quiet accumulation mode. Celsius has strong fundamentals (Grade A, 0.81 score) and DCF upside at 34.9%. Tight price action on low volume is the accumulation tell.

technical.summary: CELH is tight inside the BB channel ($27.08–$31.22). RSI 51.8 neutral. Volume drying up to 0.84x on flat price action is the classic accumulation tell. DCF at $45.79 (34.9% upside) with Grade A fundamentals creates a compelling setup if a catalyst emerges.

wyckoff_narrative: Accumulation Phase A-B with volume contraction is the setup. The tight $28.95–$30.10 range on 0.84x volume suggests supply has been absorbed. A catalyst (earnings, partnership announcement, distribution expansion) could trigger the Phase C Spring or directly into Phase D markup.

valuation.summary: 34.9% DCF upside with Grade A fundamentals and Wyckoff accumulation phase = quality setup waiting for a catalyst.

key_strengths: Grade A fundamentals with strong brand momentum in energy drinks
International expansion optionality — US success replicating globally
fundamentals.summary: Grade A fundamentals support the DCF bull case. CELH is a quality setup in quiet accumulation.

risks: No catalyst identified yet — accumulation could extend without a trigger
Energy drink market competition intensifying from Alani Nu, Ghost, etc.
